attack_chain.description: An unauthorized third party obtained AWS credentials to LogicGate's cloud storage servers. The specific method of credential compromise was not disclosed.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: on February 23 an unauthorized third party obtained credentials to its Amazon Web Services-hosted cloud storage servers

evidence_chunks.quote: on February 23 an unauthorized third party obtained credentials to its Amazon Web Services-hosted cloud storage servers
on February 23 an unauthorized third party obtained credentials to its Amazon Web Services-hosted cloud storage servers

attack_chain.description: The attacker used the compromised credentials to access customer backup files stored in AWS S3 buckets in the Risk Cloud backup environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: used by an unauthorized third party to decrypt particular files stored in AWS S3 buckets in the LogicGate Risk Cloud backup environment

evidence_chunks.quote: used by an unauthorized third party to decrypt particular files stored in AWS S3 buckets in the LogicGate Risk Cloud backup environment
used by an unauthorized third party to decrypt particular files stored in AWS S3 buckets in the LogicGate Risk Cloud backup environment

attack_chain.description: Attacker decrypted particular backup files stored in S3 (reverse of encryption — used legitimate KMS/decrypt capabilities tied to compromised creds). Mapped more precisely as unsecured data access after decryption.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: used by an unauthorized third party to decrypt particular files stored in AWS S3 buckets

evidence_chunks.quote: used by an unauthorized third party to decrypt particular files stored in AWS S3 buckets
used by an unauthorized third party to decrypt particular files stored in AWS S3 buckets

attack_chain.description: Backup files were reportedly stolen from S3, though LogicGate declined to confirm whether decrypted data was exfiltrated from their servers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Kunkel would not say, when asked, if the attacker also exfiltrated the decrypted customer data from its servers.

evidence_chunks.quote: Kunkel would not say, when asked, if the attacker also exfiltrated the decrypted customer data from its servers.
Kunkel would not say, when asked, if the attacker also exfiltrated the decrypted customer data from its servers.

decrypt particular files stored in AWS S3 buckets in the LogicGate Risk Cloud backup environment

initial_access_vector: Compromised AWS credentials obtained by an unauthorized third party (method of compromise not disclosed)
persistence_mechanism: None reported; access enabled by valid AWS credentials
